rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER PATTEE VS. PUBLIC RECREATION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re: Defendant Emilie Buckley'S Propounded Requests For Admissions, Set One And Corresponding Form Interrogatory No. 17.1; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Fabi M. Behnam, Esq.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Monday, January 27, 2025 Line 1. 6 - PLAINTIFF JENNIFER PATTEE AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND DERIVATIVELY FOR PUBLIC RECREATION, INC.'s Motion For Protective Order Re: Defendant Emilie Buckley'S Propounded Requests For Admissions, Set One And Corresponding Form Interrogatory No. 17.1; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Fabi M. Behnam, Esq. This tentative ruling applies to the seven discovery motions set for hearing on 1/27/2025. The court finds exceptional circumstances support appointment of a discovery referee for all discovery purposes. (CCP 639(a)(5); CRC 3.920(c).) (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: BAY AREA LIGHTWORKS, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. MCKEE & COMPANY ELECTRIC, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WRIT OF ATTACHMENT /Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion calendar for Monday , January 27, 2025 Line 8. PLAINTIFF BAY AREA LIGHTWORKS, INC.'s MOTION FOR WRIT OF ATTACHMENT /Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Attachment. Plaintiff Bay Area Lightworks, Inc.'s Motion for Writ of Attachment and Application For Right To Attach Order is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. The court finds Plaintiff has made the showing required by Code of Civil Procedure section 484.090(a). The court finds a writ of attachment should issue under Code of Civil Procedure section 483.010. Defendant McKee failed to demonstrate a right of exemption as to any asset subject to the attachment writ/order. Plaintiff has a right to attach property of Defendant McKee & Company Electric, Inc. in the amount of $328,348.82, which includes $266,149.94 in principal owed under the construction subcontract and $62,198.88 in interest. An undertaking of $20,000 is required before a writ shall issue. The request for a temporary restraining order is denied. Plaintiff has not made a sufficient showing to support such an order granting the requested relief at this time. In any event, the record as a whole does not demonstrate the balance of hardships favors Plaintiff or that the requested TRO is appropriate at this time. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: RED OAK REALTY VS. VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion calendar for Monday , January 27, 2025 Line 11. PLAINTIFF RED OAK REALTY's Motion For Preliminary Injunction. Plaintiff's motion for preliminary injunction is DENIED. In deciding whether to issue a preliminary injunction, the court evaluates two interrelated factors: (1) the likelihood that the party seeking the injunction will ultimately prevail on the merits; and (2) the balance of harms presented, i.e., the comparative consequences of the issuance vs. non-issuance of the injunction. (Common Cause v. Board of Superiors (1989) 49 Cal.3d 432, 441-442.) These two factors balance such that "[t]he more likely it is that plaintiffs will ultimately prevail, the less severe must be the harm that they allege will occur if the injunction does not issue." (Right Site Coalition v. Los Angeles Unified School District (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 336, 338-339.) Here, plaintiff fails to sustain its evidentiary burden and does not show a likelihood of prevailing or that the balance of equities and harms weighs in its favor. Plaintiff's moving papers are based solely on the declaration of its owner and CEO Vanessa Bergmark. She asserts that Defendants misused Plaintiff's confidential information to solicit and improperly poach Plaintiff's agents. (Bergmark Decl., pars. 14-15.) Defendants' objection to this evidence as lacking foundation/personal knowledge is sustained; declarant fails to demonstrate a basis for her assertions. In addition, Defendants present evidence that Plaintiff's former employees-not Defendants-initiated the recruitment discussions and those discussions did not involve the alleged "confidential information." (Jenssen Decl., par. 16; Mansour Decl., pars. 23-24; Weinstein Decl., pars. 10-14, 19; Einsidler Decl., pars. 6, 11.) There is no material evidence those employees did anything wrong in reaching out to Defendants. If Plaintiff had a proscription against its employees reaching out, it would likely constitute an illegal restraint of trade in violation of Business and Professions Code section 16600. (See Edwards v. Arthur Anderson LLP (2008) 44 Cal.4th 937, 945-947.) Plaintiff therefore fails to show a misuse of its alleged confidential information For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.
